title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts income tax cut proposal could decrease state revenue by $5.1 billion annually once fully implemented.
Poll shows 78% of Boston.com readers support the tax rate reduction from 5% to 4%.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the measure, citing potential negative impact on state finances and competitiveness.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church, 67, is on trial for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with ethylene glycol from antifreeze.
Prosecutors claim Church's remarks and actions, such as filming Fowler in distress, indicate premeditated murder over a love triangle.
Church's defense argues she was venting frustration in notes and filmed Fowler to show substance abuse effects, denying murder intentions.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes legislation to lift Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and allow crossbow use for all hunters.
Proposed changes seek to manage deer overpopulation, supported by MassWildlife and Mass Audubon, with reduced bowhunting setback distances.
Massachusetts and Maine are the only states enforcing a full Sunday hunting ban, an outdated blue law, according to Healey.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny discovered a derringer pistol in a sealed case while magnet fishing in a Bridgewater river.
The Cape Cod YouTuber's find has garnered significant attention online, amassing over 1.2 million Reddit views.
DeMontigny reported the discovery to police, and his YouTube channel highlights magnet fishing finds and debris removal.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old, Sergio Josue Castellanos, is charged with murder following a deadly stabbing in Chelsea.
The incident occurred near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue, leaving multiple stabbing victims, one of whom died after surgery.
The victim who died was a 25-year-old man from Chelsea; police urged witnesses to contact them as the investigation continues.

title: Two officers injured in struggle with knife-wielding man at Forest Hills station, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers were injured at Forest Hills MBTA station during a struggle with a knife-wielding suspect.
The suspect threatened people with a butcher's knife and attempted to stab bus tires before police intervened.
An officer's gun was discharged during the incident, but no one was hit; the suspect was subdued and arrested.

title: Man accused of kicking dog in Roxbury before being assaulted by group
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man in Roxbury faces animal cruelty charges for allegedly kicking a dog named Biscuit into the street.
Five unidentified individuals allegedly assaulted the suspect shortly after the dog-kicking incident.
The dog, Biscuit, remains missing; authorities urge sightings be reported to 617-704-7726.

title: Mass. man charged with threatening FBI director’s girlfriend
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden Welch Ruml, 26, from Cambridge, was charged for threatening the girlfriend of FBI Director Kash Patel.
The threatening email was sent after Ruml read an article about Alexis Wilkins' security and political affiliations.
Ruml was arrested on March 13; he pled not guilty and faces up to five years in prison if convicted.

title: Board a lobster boat and dine on your catch while staying at this Boston hotel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Lenox in Boston offers a Boston Harbor Lobster Experience Package starting May, featuring a private two-hour lobstering tour.
Guests dine on their catch with a five-course lobster menu by Chef Daniel Kenney and stay overnight at The Lenox.
The package costs $1,650 for two and $1,950 for four, including transportation, meals, and accommodations.

title: Red Line car shells cleared from customs, reviving replacement project
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: U.S. customs cleared Red Line car shells, ending nearly 10 months of detention under the Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act.
CRRC MA will restart manufacturing in Springfield in mid-April, rehiring over 140 previously furloughed employees.
190 Red Line cars remain to be completed as CRRC complies with documentation requirements to avoid further customs issues.
